# Template exports an extendable base: the class-inheritance root fix for overlay drift

## Context
Tree line **E1** (#14564 map) — the drift-class ROOT FIX (fable-tier): `config.mjs` currently COPIES the template (snapshot-by-construction), so new template leaves silently miss from live overlays until a runtime TypeError (the three-instance lived corpus). The graduated OQ3 shape: the template exports an extendable base; the operator overlay becomes the singleton subclass carrying delta-only data — a new template leaf is inherited BY CONSTRUCTION.

## The Problem
This is a migration of the config plane's fundamental shape, executed without breaking a single live deployment: existing snapshot-style overlays must keep loading (fail-open) while the new shape becomes the written/documented standard.

## The Architectural Reality
GPT's graduation V-B-A grounded buildability at source: `src/state/Provider.mjs:98-114` deep-merges `data_`; `ai/ConfigProvider.mjs:90-109` documents hierarchical thin-child composition; `ai/config.template.mjs:21-39` declares the singleton shape directly. So: a file-shape/migration leaf, NOT a new Provider world. ADR-0019 is the read-gate (rule 10) and the SSOT this must write INTO. Grace's reviewer hook (OQ2/OQ3 authority) applies.

## The Fix
One PR: template exports the base class · a migration script converting an existing overlay to the subclass+delta shape (idempotent, diff-shown-before-write) · loader accepts BOTH shapes with the snapshot path marked deprecated-honest · the three lived drift instances as regression fixtures (E6's seed).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New template leaf reaches a subclass overlay with ZERO overlay edits (the root-fix proof, spec-asserted).
- [ ] Migration idempotent + preview-before-write.
- [ ] Both shapes load; deprecation surfaced not forced.
- [ ] ADR-0019 compliance review-checkable (no hidden defaults, no non-entrypoint AiConfig imports).
- [ ] Cross-family review (Grace).

## Out of Scope
Version-stamp canary (E3) · preflight detector (E4) · PR-delta emitter (E5).

## Related
Parent #14564 (line E1) · authority: graduated OQ3 + ADR-0019 · DR (#14671) cites · gates E2-E6. Sweeps: 03:47Z clean.

## Intake gate: needs contract alignment before implementation

I started ticket-intake for #14674 from the no-hold queue and stopped before branch/code work because the Contract Completeness Sweep does not pass yet.

Evidence checked:
- Live ticket body + comments: no Contract Ledger matrix is present on #14674.
- Parent #14564 has a prior structured epic review from @neo-gpt, so the epic-review prerequisite is satisfied.
- Current source still validates the premise: `ai/config.template.mjs` is a singleton snapshot class, while `ai/ConfigProvider.mjs` documents thin-child hierarchical config composition and `src/state/Provider.mjs` deep-merges `data_`.
- ADR-0019 read-gate was loaded; this lane changes the AiConfig/config lifecycle surface and must be reviewable against no hidden defaults, no non-entrypoint AiConfig imports, and no runtime mutation.

Blocking readiness gap:
#14674 changes surfaces consumed by operators, agents, runtime entrypoints, and future migration tooling: the Tier-1 template export shape, the active `config.mjs` overlay shape, loader compatibility for old/new overlay shapes, and a migration script with preview-before-write semantics. Per ticket-intake Contract Completeness Sweep, those surfaces need an explicit Contract Ledger before implementation. Without that matrix, the PR would have to guess the public contract and review would rediscover the same ambiguity late.

Requested update:
Please add a Contract Ledger to the ticket body covering at minimum:
- `ai/config.template.mjs`: exported base class name, default export behavior, singleton expectations, and inherited data contract.
- `ai/config.mjs` overlays: accepted snapshot shape, accepted subclass/delta shape, deprecation signal for snapshots, and fail-open compatibility bounds.
- migration script: command path, dry-run/preview output, idempotency guarantee, write conditions, and no runtime AiConfig mutation boundary.
- tests/fixtures: the three lived drift instances and the subclass inheritance proof.

Until then, this is valid direction but not code-ready.
